# komodo_network (Resource)

Creates a docker network on a Komodo-managed server via `docker network create {name}`. Changing `server` or `name` forces a new resource. Note: there is no Komodo API endpoint to delete networks, so destroying this resource is a no-op on the server.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "komodo_network" "example" {
  server_id = komodo_server.example.id
  name      = "my-network"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the docker network to create. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `server_id` (String) The server ID or name on which to create the network. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The resource identifier in the form `server:name`.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the format: {server_id}:{network_name}
terraform import komodo_network.example 6627c3e4f1a2b3c4d5e6f7a8:my-network
```
